violations.claim: VIOLATION: smt2 field is prop.smt2 verbatim; later contract's mutations are absent —
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 121
; VIOLATION: smt2 field is prop.smt2 verbatim; later contract's mutations are absent —
;            the check replays the original proof and can never return sat (violation)

(declare-const smt2_is_earlier_verbatim Int)   ; 1 = pushed smt2 == prop.smt2 unchanged
(declare-const later_mutations_in_smt2 Int)    ; 1 = later contract's effects appear in formula
(declare-const check_can_return_sat Int)       ; 1 = check could detect a P2 violation

; Transition: smt2 field assigned as `prop.smt2` — no composition with later contract
(assert (= smt2_is_earlier_verbatim 1))

; Transition: later contract is only referenced in targetContract string label, not in smt2
(assert (=> (= smt2_is_earlier_verbatim 1) (= later_mutations_in_smt2 0)))

; Transition: a formula with no later-state variables cannot constrain post-mutation state
(assert (=> (= later_mutations_in_smt2 0) (= check_can_return_sat 0)))

; Violation: the check structure guarantees the original proof still holds
(assert (= check_can_return_sat 0))

(check-sat)
